free_text: Spin-flip transitions in O-16 and O-18 excited by
inelastic proton scattering.
The Orsay synchrocyclotron.
The gas cells were operated at pressures of
about 3-4 atm. The target thickness was checked
regularly by measuring the (p,p) scattering on the
hydrogen target. The deduced target thickness for the
oxygen gas ranged between 7.5 and 10.0 mg/cm
Absolute normalization of the cross sections is
obtained by comparison with the known p-p scattering
cross section at 15. degr., using a polyethylene target
No any information.
Microscopic distorted-wave Born approximation.
The error in the detector efficiency
No information about source of uncertainty
Some data have been corrected.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents. Several Subents were
merged. Data were re-digitized. Subents 020, 021 added.
Natural oxygen gas was used
denotes the centroid of a broad structure.
